top_signals.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Boosted Inflation Expectations, Investment
top_signals.summary: The war in Iran caused a spike in Canadian inflation expectations and is leading the country’s oil producers to boost their investment and production plans.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
top_signals.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Ukraine pushes civil drone drive with Japan and Taiwan
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Two Millennium Trading Pods Made About $3.7 Billion Last Month
top_signals.summary: Two teams focused on trading index changes at Millennium Management made billions of dollars between them in June, lifting the multistrategy hedge fund’s monthly gains.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
